verdict: Release 2 control-surface components are merged, but app-level SaaS readiness is not accepted. Before further Release 2 implementation, KISS PM must complete RELEASE_2_APP_FOUNDATION_RESET.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers route navigation, reload/readback, permission state, and product-owner smoke.
acceptance: Real app route model replaces anchor-only navigation; reload/readback and permission state work on protected routes.
non_scope: Continuing component demo wall as application architecture
risks: Current Release 2 E2E-R2-001..010 does not prove real route model.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers dev-user select, route guard, reload/readback, permission denial, and sign out/switch user.
acceptance: User enters through a real login/dev-auth route, does not edit fixture ids by hand, and reload/readback preserves permission-aware session state.
verification: backend denial proof for unauthenticated or read-only mutation
risks: Query-param-only auth keeps the app in test harness territory.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers role landing, route reload/readback, permission-specific widgets, and next action.
acceptance: After login, route lands on a role-appropriate dashboard with reload/readback and permission-aware next action.
non_scope: Decorative executive BI dashboard without actions
verification: Admin, PM, resource manager, and read-only role landing smoke
risks: A single generic demo page hides role workflow gaps.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers route navigation, selected nav, reload/readback, permission state, and no anchor-only page scroll.
acceptance: App shell uses real page routes, selected navigation reflects route, and reload/readback plus permission state work for each page.
risks: Existing navigation links are anchors and do not prove SaaS shell quality.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers demo reset, route reload/readback, seeded project availability, and permission state.
acceptance: Demo data can be reset and read back through product-visible flow; route reload/readback and permission denials remain deterministic.
non_scope: Manual API seeding before normal demo use
risks: Manual seeding makes product-owner demo non-repeatable.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers KPI settings route, preview, publish, audit, reload/readback, and read-only permission denial.
acceptance: KPI setup is a real route with shadcn/Radix form/table/dialog primitives, preview, audit, reload/readback, and permission denial.
risks: Current KPI threshold UI is embedded in demo panels, not a page flow.

rows.requirement: Project/Gantt page flow without manual API seeding
required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers seeded project Gantt route, reload/readback, permission state, and no manual API seeding.
acceptance: User opens a seeded project Gantt through a real route; reload/readback and permission-disabled edit state work without manual API calls.
risks: Current Gantt defaults and anchors do not prove product page readiness.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers resource page route, overload signal, reload/readback, permission state, and next action.
acceptance: Resource capacity is a real page route with clear next action, reload/readback, and permission-specific resolution state.
risks: Heatmap alone can look like a component demo without route and workflow context.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers portfolio/KPI route navigation, action preview, audit, reload/readback, and permission denial.
acceptance: Portfolio and KPI pages expose governed action flow with route reload/readback and permission state, not just stitched component evidence.
risks: Old R2 component E2E can pass while product page journey remains absent.

rows.requirement: Read-only user experience and backend denial proof
required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers read-only route access, disabled reasons, reload/readback, permission state, and backend denial.
acceptance: Read-only user sees useful route/page context; reload/readback works; permission denial is proven in backend/action path.
verification: Read-only UI and direct backend mutation denial
risks: UI-only disabled buttons are insufficient acceptance evidence.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 Product owner smoke proves login/dev-user select -> role landing -> real pages -> profile/account -> settings/admin -> KPI preview/publish/readback/audit -> seeded Gantt -> resource/portfolio signal -> reload/deep link/back-forward -> read-only denial.
acceptance: Product-owner smoke follows real routes, reload/readback, permission denial, and no manual fixture-id editing.
non_scope: Counting E2E-R2-001..010 alone as app-readiness evidence
risks: Smoke can become superficial if it only clicks components and checks text.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 must pass before Release 2 implementation resumes; route reload/readback and permission proof are mandatory.
acceptance: Release 2 remains blocked until R2R-001..R2R-015 are done; route, reload/readback, permission, profile, settings, seed, and product-owner smoke evidence exist.
non_scope: Claiming product readiness from component/control-surface evidence only
risks: Release 2 implementation can continue incorrectly unless this row explicitly blocks Release 2 resume.

rows.requirement: User profile/account menu and personal preferences shell
required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers profile/account menu, preferences, role/tenant readback, route reload/readback, permission state, and dev sign out/switch user.
acceptance: App shell route shows avatar/name/current role; profile/account menu opens profile and preferences; reload/readback and permission state are clear.
non_scope: Full persisted preference engine if placeholder reason is explicit
risks: Current user chip/testUser text is not a real account flow.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers settings/admin route, shadcn/Radix tables/forms/dialogs/sheets, KPI preview, audit, reload/readback, and permission denial.
acceptance: Dedicated settings/admin area uses shadcn/Radix primitives by default, preview before risky apply, audit/readback, route reload, and permission guards.
non_scope: Scattered admin demo panels as product settings
risks: Existing admin panels are powerful but scattered and not a SaaS settings area.

required_e2e: E2E-R2R-001 covers real routes, deep links, back-forward, reload/readback, route guards, permission state, and selected navigation.
acceptance: Required routes support deep links, browser back-forward, reload, route guards, readback, permission state, and no unrelated surface scrolling.
risks: Vite fallback can mask missing route model because every path renders the same long page.
